name: reasoning-cot-control
description: Apply Reasoning Models Struggle to Control Chains of Thought to reasoning problems. Technique: Chain-of-thought (CoT) monitoring is a promising tool for detecting misbehaviors...
paperTitle: Reasoning Models Struggle to Control Chains of Thought
content:
## Overview

This skill implements the technique from "Reasoning Models Struggle to Control Chains of Thought".

Key contribution: Chain-of-thought (CoT) monitoring is a promising tool for detecting misbehaviors and understanding the motivations of modern reasoning models. However, if models can control what they verbalize in their CoT, it could undermine CoT monitorability.

## When to use

- Apply when solving reasoning problems
- When baseline approaches are insufficient
- Working with structured problem spaces

## When NOT to use

- If problem constraints limit complexity
- When simpler methods achieve target performance
- Time-critical scenarios with strict latency needs

## Core Technique

The paper proposes an approach through:

1. Problem formulation from the paper
2. Algorithm implementation as described
3. Integration with existing systems
4. Evaluation against baselines

## Implementation

1. Understand problem requirements
2. Implement core algorithm components
3. Configure evaluation metrics from paper
4. Compare against baseline methods
5. Tune hyperparameters for your domain

## References

Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.05706
